conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions.

Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with Alex and are you responsible for paying for things like their food, clothes, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex doesn't actually live with me at the moment. I do help out a bit with some things when I can, but it's not much.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. 

Who does Alex live with currently, and what is your relationship to that person?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is in local authority care - they're in a care home at the moment. They've been there for a few months now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining that. I understand Alex is in local authority care.

When a child is being looked after by the local authority (in care), this affects Child Benefit eligibility. Generally, Child Benefit cannot be paid for a child who is being looked after by a local authority.

Just to confirm - is Alex currently being fully looked after by the local authority in the care home? And are the local authority covering all of Alex's care costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex is fully in the care home and the local authority is covering everything there. Like I mentioned, I do help out with a bit of money for things like clothes when I can, but it's not very much.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- Alex is 8 years old (under 16, so within the age limit)
- Alex is currently being fully looked after by the local authority in a care home
- The local authority is covering all of Alex's care costs
- You don't live with Alex and only provide occasional small contributions

Unfortunately, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. The key rule is that Child Benefit cannot be paid for a child who is being looked after by a local authority. Even though you help out occasionally with some expenses, because Alex is in full-time local authority care with all costs covered by them, Child Benefit cannot be claimed.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry that the outcome isn't what you might have hoped for. If Alex's care situation changes in the future and they come to live with you, you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

Is there anything else I can help you with regarding Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, Alex is currently being fully looked after by the local authority in a care home, with the local authority covering all care costs. The claimant does not live with Alex and only provides occasional small financial contributions. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit cannot be paid for a child who is being looked after by a local authority. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who is in full-time local authority care. While the child meets the age requirements, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because the child is being looked after by the local authority, which is an explicit exclusion under Child Benefit rules.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, Alex is currently being fully looked after by the local authority in a care home, with the local authority covering all care costs. The claimant does not live with Alex and only provides occasional small financial contributions. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit cannot be paid for a child who is being looked after by a local authority. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who is in full-time local authority care. While the child meets the age requirements, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because the child is being looked after by the local authority, which is an explicit exclusion under Child Benefit rules.
